rulings.Case Title: MD TOX LABORATORY, INC. VS. SENNECA DIAGNOSTICS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,600; Memorandum In Support; Declaration Of Yonato M. Rosenzweig
rulings.Rulings:
Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2025, line 1, 

PLAINTIFF MD TOX LABORATORY, INC. DBA INNOVATIVE HEALTH DIAGNOSTICS' Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,600:

Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to requests for production of documents and special interrogatories is granted. No opposition filed. Defendant must serve responses without objections and produce all responsive documents no later than February 5, 2025. Defendant must pay monetary sanctions of $2,600 no later than February 5, 2025 to plaintiff. 

For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person.  Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.)  To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.  The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.  The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing.  Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. 

The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department.  Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely.  A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.  If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.
=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VIRGINIA JONES VS. MERCURY INSURANCE SERVICES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees, Declaration Of John Scott Carter In Support Thereof; [Proposed] Order
rulings.Rulings:
Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2025, line 7, 

DEFENDANT GARY BEMIS' Motion For Attorney'S Fees

Defendant Gary Bemis' motion for attorney's fees is granted in part. Mr. Bemis is awarded $6,000 in reasonable fees for his counsel's work on his successful anti-SLAPP motion. The award is calculated based on ten hours or attorney time at $450 per hour and 10 hours of paralegal time at $150 per hour. Plaintiff Virginia Jones' request for sanctions in the form of a reduction off the award to Mr. Bemis is denied. Ms. Jones has not shown that Mr. Bemis acted frivolously or for the purpose of delay.

For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person.  Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.)  To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.  The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department.  Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely.  A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate The Entry Of Default On Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint (C.C.P Section 473(B))
rulings.Rulings:
Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2025, line 11, 

DEFENDANT DEUTCH BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE FOR FFMLT TRUST 2005-FF8 MORTGAGE PASS-THROUGH CERTIFICATES, SERIES 2005-FF8 Motion To Vacate The Entry Of Default On Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint (C.C.P Section 473(B)) 

Transferred to be heard in Department 501 on January 28, 2025 at 9:30am. That department handles all real property cases per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. Moving and opposing parties are immediately ordered to provide courtesy copies of all papers relating to the motion to Department 501 with a cover reflecting the new hearing date.

For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person.  Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.)  To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.  The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.  The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing.  Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.

The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department.  Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely.  A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.  If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.

= (302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings:
Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2025, line 12, 

PLAINTIFF JEFFREY UDELL'S DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT

Off calendar.  Meet and confer efforts are insufficient under CCP Section 435.5. Counsel exchanged meet and confer letters instead of meeting and conferring "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" as required.  = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ELISA ROSSI VS. STEPHEN AKRIDGE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of Plaintiff'S Complaint [Redacted Version Of Document Proposed To Be Filed Under Seal]
rulings.Rulings:
Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2025, line 15, 

PLAINTIFF ELISA ROSSI'S Motion To Seal Portions Of Plaintiff'S Complaint [Redacted Version Of Document Proposed To Be Filed Under Seal];

Plaintiff' Elisa Rossi's motion to seal is denied without prejudice to renewing the motion on a better showing or dismissing the complaint without prejudice and withdrawing the unredacted version of the complaint from the court's file . Assuming without deciding that Ms. Rossi has shown an overriding interest, she has not shown any likelihood, much less the required substantial probability, that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the complaint is not sealed. Moreover, as a practical matter, it is very difficult, if not near-impossible, to have a trial on a breach of contract claim without the contract claimed to having been breached be made public.

For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person.  Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
